name: flow-idempotency-key-survives-resume
description: contracts/adapter-pack.md 'Idempotency-key injection' rules 3 and 5, at the flow level: an adapter-injected key rides a step's `running` record (this scenario drives `execute_step_with_idempotency_key` directly, the JSON-conformance twin of the hand-written crash-mid-effect unit test in crates/keel-core/tests/idempotency.rs), but it never feeds the step key. Run 1's step 1 completes with key ik-1, then the flow crashes. On resume, step 1 is substituted from the journal — its live effect (which would return a different payload) never fires, EVEN THOUGH the resumed call injects a different key (ik-should-be-ignored): the injected key is not part of args_hash, so it cannot fence replay. `expect_recorded_key: null` before each step pins that the peek only ever surfaces a key for a crashed (still-`running`) record, never a fresh or a terminal one. Step 2 then runs live for the first time, carrying its own key (ik-2), and the flow completes. Stubs skip tier-2 scenarios; the real core runs this in crates/keel-core/tests/flows_conformance.rs.
